question: In internal fragmentation, memory is internal to a partition and ____________
A solution to the problem of external fragmentation is ____________
Another solution to the problem of external fragmentation problem is to ____________

options.a: permit the logical address space of a process to be noncontiguous
options.b: permit smaller processes to be allocated memory at last
options.c: permit larger processes to be allocated memory at last

question: If relocation is static and is done at assembly or load time, compaction _________
The disadvantage of moving all process to one end of memory and all holes to the other direction, producing one large hole of available memory is ____________

options.a: first fit, best fit, worst fit
options.b: best fit, first fit, worst fit
options.c: worst fit, best fit, first fit

options.a: enough total memory exists to satisfy a request but it is not contiguous
options.b: the total memory is insufficient to satisfy a request
options.c: a request cannot be satisfied even when the total memory is free

question: External fragmentation will not occur when?

options.d: no matter which algorithm is used, it will always occur

question: Sometimes the overhead of keeping track of a hole might be ____________
When the memory allocated to a process is slightly larger than the process, then ____________

options.c: both internal and external fragmentation occurs
options.d: neither internal nor external fragmentation occurs
